title: Corpus manifest record v2 (one JSON object per line; lanes write MANIFEST.<lane>.jsonl)
description: One immutable record per stored byte version, written once at fetch time. Clearance is NOT stored here: it is computed from clear/restrict/reject/takedown events in the access log (staging is a state, not a place — files are written to their final path and must not be parsed until effective clearance is 'cleared'). Refetches create new content_version records; paths are never reused.

content_version.description: vN-<first 8 hex of sha256 of local_path bytes>

supersedes.description: Prior content_version (null for v1); also emit a supersede event

raw_path.description: Raw response bytes (+headers sidecar) when local_path is a derived snapshot

transformation.description: Tool+version deriving local_path from raw bytes

ocr.description: True if text layer is OCR-derived; low-confidence OCR requires human review before cataloging

sha256.description: Hash of local_path bytes — verified against disk by the tool

fetch_event.description: FK → access-log fetch/refetch event; tool cross-checks action, source_id, doc_id, content_version, sha256, local_path

nation_authored.description: True if the issuing entity is a Tribal Nation — such documents always require a human/* clear event
